commits: 9f404c7: test(transparency): RED — streaming served-by vertex_ai_beta + AI-Studio-vs-Vertex disambiguation
e2c9ffa: fix(transparency): normalize streaming served-by — vertex_ai_beta + AI-Studio-vs-Vertex by api_base_host

step1_observations.finding: api_base host is a reliable AI-Studio-vs-Vertex disambiguator on the streaming path.
details: vertex_and_google_ai_studio_gemini.py:2935 and :3227 construct CustomStreamWrapper with custom_llm_provider='vertex_ai_beta' HARDCODED — for both AI-Studio and Vertex gemini, so the wrapper instance attr always leaks vertex_ai_beta on streams.
get_api_base.py:106-113: custom_llm_provider='gemini' (the gemini/ prefix, no vertex_location/project) → host generativelanguage.googleapis.com (AI Studio).

Empirical scratch (deleted before commit): AI-Studio stream api_base = https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-3.5-flash:generateContent ; Vertex stream api_base = us-east5-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/proj/locations/us-east5/publishers/google/models/gemini-1.5-flash:generateContent

fix_summary: Added _normalize_served_provider(provider, api_base_host): maps vertex_ai_beta→vertex_ai, and for the Google/Vertex family resolves to gemini when api_base_host == generativelanguage.googleapis.com (AI Studio); else stays vertex_ai (gateway). Applied in attribute_served_backend after api_base_host is computed, before classify_backend_kind, so streaming (wrapper attr) and non-streaming (_hidden_params) converge. Added vertex_ai_beta to _GATEWAY_PROVIDERS so classify_backend_kind handles it defensively. Additive only — no ServedBackend fields, signatures, or serializers changed.
agent_verify_result: pass — tests/test_served_attribution.py 20 passed; tests/test_served_attribution.py+test_header_safety.py 32 passed; -k 'served or transparency or header' 120 passed, 1 skipped; ruff check + format clean
